channel.title: Pentagon on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Pentagon on Alien Truth Social

item.title: New Emails Reveal Pentagon Effort to Align Messaging on AATIP and Luis Elizondo
item.description:
Newly released Pentagon emails from May 2019 reveal an internal contradiction regarding Luis Elizondo’s role in the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP). A senior Pentagon official, Neill Tipton, stated in an email that Elizondo had no assigned responsibilities related to AATIP during his time under Tipton. However, other emails show Tipton was directly communicating with Elizondo about AATIP and reviewing a memo concerning responsibilities for it. Pentagon officials expressed concern about these contradictions and the need for consistent messaging. Despite Tipton’s 2019 statement, previously released records show him engaging in direct discussions with Elizondo about AATIP responsibilities in 2017. Luis Elizondo disputes Tipton’s characterization, stating that documentary evidence shows Tipton was aware of his involvement and agreed to assume his responsibilities.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/new-emails-reveal-pentagon-effort-to-align-messaging-on-aatip-and-luis-elizondo

item.title: Newly Released Pentagon Records Center on UAP and Internal Email Communications
item.description:
A collection of Department of Defense records has been released, focusing on internal email communications involving Pentagon spokesperson Christopher Sherwood and references to Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP). These documents stem from a Freedom of Information Act request seeking insight into how UAP-related discussions occurred within the Pentagon’s public affairs office. The request specifically targeted emails sent to or from Sherwood containing keywords like “UAP.” While 416 pages were found responsive, portions were withheld under FOIA exemptions related to intelligence information and internal deliberations. The Black Vault has filed an appeal regarding the extensive redactions.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/pentagon-spokesperson-christopher-sherwood-emails-on-ufos-uap-and-more

item.title: Archive of Luis Elizondo’s ‘Deleted’ Emails
item.description:
Luis Elizondo is a central figure in discussions about Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP) due to his alleged involvement with the Pentagon’s Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP). The Black Vault reported that the Department of Defense (DoD) destroyed Elizondo’s emails, hindering verification of his claims. To circumvent this, The Black Vault pursued FOIA requests for emails of individuals who likely corresponded with Elizondo, indirectly recovering some exchanges. This archive contains these recovered emails, as well as records where “no records” were found, offering insight into Elizondo’s interactions within the DoD.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/archive-of-luis-elizondos-deleted-emails

item.title: Closed-Door 2024 UAP Briefing: FOIA Discloses Select Media Invitees
item.description:
Newly released records obtained through a Freedom of Information Act request reveal journalists who were privately invited to an embargoed Pentagon media roundtable on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP). This event, held on March 6, 2024, was connected to the Department of Defense’s first volume of the congressionally mandated Historical Record Report on U.S. government UAP programs. The invitation, sent by Pentagon spokesperson Susan Gough, outlined strict conditions for participation, including agreeing to an embargo until the report’s public release on March 8. Journalists from outlets such as The New York Times, CNN, Politico, Task & Purpose, and The Washington Post were among those invited, though some recipients remain redacted due to privacy concerns.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/closed-door-2024-uap-briefing-foia-discloses-select-media-invitees

item.title: Yankee Blue: Part of Pentagon UFO Disinformation Campaign
item.description:
Yankee Blue was a fake United States Air Force program that purported to involve the reverse-engineering of alien spacecraft. In reality, it was an internal hazing and misinformation campaign, possibly part of a broader Pentagon disinformation program. For decades, personnel in classified Air Force units were shown “UFO” photos and told they were assisting in a top-secret alien-technology recovery program. This ritual persisted from the Cold War era until its discontinuation in 2023. The strategy likely originated as a security measure to protect sensitive programs and divert attention from advanced weapons development.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yankee_Blue
